name: Townhouse Siam Privi Hotel
detail: My girlfriend and I stayed for more than 4 week, because the price and value was the best on Agoda. + best price (450 THB) + big (35 sm) bright rooms + led TV + new comfortable mattresses + very friendly stuff + 15 minutes walk to MRT Hui Kwang + lots of cheep massage, services and food around + night market Hui Kwang has a lot of good fruits and vegetables for best price + no smoking rooms + quite place =============================== - no sightseeing - if you don't like to walk, you should take a taxi or bike everytime you go somewhere =============================== After all, one of the best hotels for such price, no doubt we will recommend it, and enjoy it on our next visit to Bangkok

name: Solaria Nishitetsu Hotel Bangkok
detail: My room was spotless, with a cozy bed and great city views.

name: Palette The Travellers Hotel Bangkok
detail: It was great experience to stay with the property.

name: Ruamchitt Plaza Hotel
detail: OK, it is rare to have a hotel in bangkok where smoking is allowed and it was quite a stay for us. It was very quiet hotel too so you can sleep well at night. It was not a bad hotel at all. For that price, I think it is all worthwhile. I would give five stars if they gave me bottle water the first day when I checked in, so next time, give me water! LOL... Roberson is nearby and so as all transportation. If you are looking to snack shopping, Roberson can fulfill all your snack needs. There were quite a lot of nice restaurants nearby and across the road as well. And there is an old book store nearby and it was a germ! I would definitely stay in this hotel next time. My suggestion, probably better ventilation before check in, it was quite hot in bangkok with the sun shine directly, no amount of air condition can cool the room down in an instant. Also, better television, it was almost impossible to watch anything, the reception was very bad.

name: Siam Stadium Hostel
detail: This hostel is newly built in 2018. Drop off at skytrain National Stadium stn and go straight around 700 meter. It is in central Bangkok. It is also near Hua Lum Pong railway station by taxi not over 100 thb. Breakfast is the most thing I like because it is not only bread and coffee, but there is rice and thai food for eat with rice. Delicious. Recommend to stay here

name: Evergreen Place Siam by UHG
detail: i think this is one of the best hotels in bangkok in the very affordable range. the rooms are very spacious, even with kitchennette facilities. sauna, steamer and pool are simply great. massage facilities have very professional people working there (probably the best massage we had in thailand). and, most importantly, there buffet breakfast is absolutely delicious and we really liked there attention to detail. the staff speaks english and is very professional. the hotel located 50 m from ratchasima train station and 10 min walk from siam square and siam paragon shopping centre.

name: Siam Eco Hostel
detail: This was the final of the six places I stayed in Thailand whilst traveling around the country . I stayed here for nearly a week. This place is newly re-furnished and thrives because of its staff and location. The staff are great and look after you as if you are family. They give great information on both local and national places of interest and also drew me out maps on occasions on how to get to places! Spending the late evenings learning Thai at reception (well counting to 5) was an added bonus as well as the shopping/markets/ travelling advice that I was given. The rooms were clean and have lockers. The location is very central with a BTS station less than a minute walk away that takes you to major places as did the boat that I took. The hostel is also in a great location to get the BTS 40 minute train to/from the international airport. This was a great place to stay and if I come back to Bangkok again, this is where I will be based. Thank you.

name: Galleria 10 Hotel Bangkok
detail: I really enjoyed my stay here and am already booked to go back two times. This hotel is a splurge for me since I am traveling for a year but I still think it's a great value and so nice to have a modern clean cool room to come back to in Bangkok. After not having wifi for 3 weeks I literally sat in my room and got caught up with my writing and planning. I did think the food was expensive, and the mini fridge too, but I think it goes with a nice hotel in Bangkok. And it didn't stop me from utilizing both. The staff was lovely and let me use the pool for a few hours after I checked out. The location is great for cheap street food but the hotel has amazing food too. The buffet lunch is pricey for one person but really good. Nice huge television and very comfortable bed. Best sleep I have had in months.

name: The Quarter Ladprao by UHG
detail: This is an amazing hotel I’d rate it 5 stars. So clean and lovely pool and and nice restaurant. Little bit outside Bangkok but grab is cheap and there’s also a station at union circle centre. The rooms are spacious and brand new. Can’t fault it. Only thing is I’d say they can improve breakfast by separating meat and pork for Muslim and other vegetarian guests.

name: Top High Airport Link Hotel
detail: The best hotel in bangkok (for a first timer). The location is really good with the nearest BTS station which is Phaya Thai. The staffs were really helpful in any way that they can help! They also provide shuttle service to the BTS station and also Partunam Market, although you have to walk a bit after that. The room was quite comfortable and for a 4 days stay, we are really satisfied with the rooms. They also provide gym and swimming pool, if you are someone who needs to workout even when you are travelling. In short, the best experience a hotel can provide can be found here! Highly recommended.
